Sarah is the key. The Terminator is the star, but Sarah has always been the main character. That's why it was stupid to kill her off before Terminator 3. Her evolution from a helpless to badass was the crucial, human component of the first two movies. This is her story. 

In re-watching the first Terminator, I now realize that Reese had to die. He wasn't just John's father, but the one that taught and
inspired Sarah to become "the legend." His death was the key event that hardened Sarah forever and transformed her into the "mother of the future."

Nah. Could have saved that line at least. I'll be back ? For what. You should really stop tarnishing the franchise. Names of Cameroon, Schwarzenegger, and Sarah coming back all seem just desparate measures for getting some fans into the theatres. The way terminator 2 was done, when you could feel the pain when Arnie lost his arm, that's what I'd take any day over a horde of robots beings smashed, things on fire and explosions.

This movie wasn't that bad, honestly. I get what they were trying to do with this movie. Even if Skynet doesn't exist anymore, something equivalent to it will still exist. Humans will still die but there will always be someone who will rise against Skynet or the equivalent of it, even if it isn't John Connor.

The characters are okay, except for Daniella and maybe Grace. Dani sudden change makes no sense to me. I think they could've developed her character more but they ran out of time. Grace was a bit odd... I don't know how to describe her. It felt like she had romantic feelings for Dani instead of something akin to mother-daughter relationship that the movie was going to portray. They could've shown a flashback where Dani decided that Grace should be sent back to the past and show how they interact with each other so that the audience could understand their relationship and motivation better. Instead of doing that, they decided to make that Daniella is the one who saved them all in the future as vague as possible but they failed since it was actually obvious from the start. What a wasted opportunity. I guess my major problem with both of them is that I can't understand their characters.

I think the worst thing about the movie is the action scenes. At first it was okay and it looks fun since people are getting thrown everywhere. Then you realise how awful the CGI is.It looks much more obvious when both Grace and the Rev 9 got sent back to the past and materialize from the blue orb thingy. It looks like it came from a Sci-Fi fan film on Youtube. Other than that, the action scenes are the just the same thing throughout the movie. There's not much variety to it.

Overall, this movie wasn't bad but it wasn't good either. I could understand the story and I actually enjoyed it. The characters are a bit.... odd at times but they're not forgettable. At the very least, this movie wasn't a shitshow like Ghostbuster 2016 where the feminism are obvious. In Terminator Dark Fate, there are certain parts that shows hints of feminism but they don't really shove it in your face and I really appreciate that. Now, let's wait for another reboot/remake of a popular franchise and see if it becomes a shitshow or a good movie.
@Appy Ammer Well, you make a fair point. I guess I just don't hate this movie as much as I hate Ghostbusters 2016 and Captain Marvel.

It opened today in the UK. I saw it.  No spoilers ahead. It's a trillion times better than the monstrosity that was Genishit. Linda Hamilton is great. Arnold still looks ripped. The finale is very satisfying and exciting.

On the downside, it's shot on digital video, which I know some people don't care about, but it means it doesn't have the cinematic texture of T1, T2 and even T3. And the action is pure CGI. There isn't a single real explosion in the whole film.

So yeah it's enjoyable and it's great having Linda Hamilton back. But it doesn't come close to the greatness of T2.

I won't lie, when I saw Terminator Salvation I thought that it was the coolest movie with the merch here and there including the ios game, next came Terminator Genisys.  I won't lie, that wasn't too bad but it could've been better.  Then the trailer for the 6th installment of the franchise is here and I have to question if it will be any good.    All I will say is that the cg looks very promising, however I would recommend that Terminator Dark Fate's premise be clear and come to an end concluding the franchise.    Movies are your job, I get it, but you have to understand that this is 2019, in which that doing a remake of something (I have a feeling that this will most likely happen right after this movie comes out) essentially ruins the original image when we think about "The Terminator!"  and perhaps right after this people will be talking about it in more negative regards rather than positive re-enforcement.  Criticism is vital to improving something, but don't confuse criticism with Political Correctness either, there aren't any laws in place that will somehow get you thrown in the slammer all because "you didn't represent the marginalized" or some other ridiculous complaint in the eyes of the SJWs, the worst case scenario is that you just get hate mail and that's it.   Be like Japan and laugh it off. 

(Proper context:   There was a poster promoting blood donations and on said poster is an animated character -- fully clothed and DD cup size mind you -- who's purpose is to get the attention of the consumer so that they read on the red cross poster regarding blood donations.  And the SJWs came running in and said that the poster was somehow "offensive", Japan laughs this off while if this were to happen in America it would be different: we bend to their will and for what?  The country will-- actually I'm getting off point here, my apologies.)

Most importantly, do not be like Disney and ruin the original works.   A remaster would be great, but a remake should never be pulled off.  And this is a trend that I noticed not too long ago.

With all this said, I want to say have a wonderful day Paramount Pictures (and staff too, they're humans too! :) )

Meh, it's difficult to tell where they're going with this. Cameron showed he still has it with Alita Battle Angel, so he hasn't George Lucas'd so to speak. On the other hand David Goyer is head writer, which means it'll mainly be a mindless action flick. The story also is repeating many of the same pitfalls the last two Terminator films made. How many new concepts or ideas do you see here? I can't think of much. You have the liquid terminator from T2, you have the good T-800 which seems to be a father figure, kind of like the one from Genysis. You have a Terminator who thinks they're human, like in Salvation. You also have the whole "you didn't stop judgement day, just delayed it" from T3. They're also protecting a child from a mindless killing machine that's unstoppable, which has been the plot of every terminator film. So it's already rehashing ideas even from the less popular films. They even brought back Sarah Connor. The lack of original compelling plot ideas is what has crushed this franchise, and I just don't get why this film is even being made.

I'll try to explain. Yes you are right, Terminator franchise just like the Alien one had always strong female protagonits and noone had any problem with it for two reasons: 1. They got to that position naturally and believably in-universe (in-story) and they had to earn it (the journey of the slightly airhead waitress Sarah Connor between the beginning of the first movie and the end of the second where she is a muscular ruthless figther was a VERY tough one). Even the evil female terminator in the third movie was quite logical: as an infiltration unit she enjoyed much more success and wider options than an aggresive looking muscular hulk with austrian accent.
2. It was a simple artistic decision which as I wrote above was logical in-universe. There was no political pressure behind it. The current Hollywood dictate has all the characteristics of a totalitarian regime's propaganda tool (and I know the trope very well, lived my whole childhood in a communist central european country). That slender tomboyish fembot is laughable, she had been "enhanced" to make at least some semblance of believability in her presentation as an EQUAL opponent of A TERMINATOR. Which is a laughable premise. The terminator franchise is build around the premise that humans aren't equal opponents to terminators and must rely on a combination of delay tactics, external help, smarts and a lot of luck. And the whole ensamble in this movie consists of who knows why important Dani Ramos, the tomboy fembot and Sarah. The T-800 is relegated to a role of an obsolete supporting character as a condescendingly tolerated relics of a by-gone era. Not in-universe but in current political climate.

i keep seeing every new trailer and it still looks like a pile of dog dump. It looks like a total wreckage. This is one film franchise that should be terminated already. I don't why it hasn't been? Terminator Genesis underperform domestically, i dunno why they are doing another one. Linda Hamilton is nice to see being back, but that's not enough for me to give this trailer or concept or franchise a pass at this point. It's long past due, it's one of those cinematic franchises that has gotten way past itself way by date, it's expired, and needs to be thrown out in the trash. The new trailer does not get enough to get me interested in the movie, it looks like CGI overload.

The new terminator looks like he's totally forgettable, i know some people are like "that cool scene where he cuts up people", sure it's kind of cool but where's his personality and his charisma? Even when Arnold and Robert Patrick were playing robots they had personality and more charisma than the new guy. The trailer is trying to make this terminator look cool but fails at that.

Arnold is just gonna be a regular guy, it's their take on the deleted scene in T3 but nowhere near as a parody as that one and it's taken more seriously here. I guess John Conner is gonna be killed in the first 5 minutes, why kill him? you already killed him in Genesis. It's nothing special just to have a cameo by Edward.

And James Cameron coming back? so? he praised Genesis which is one of the worst films i've seen in 2015 and i believe he was paid to say that and hasn't done anything worth a damn in years. Why should i be hyped? Just because James Cameron is involved? him being involved back in the franchise doesn't fix the problem, after T2 this franchise was troubling. This is a franchise that was spiraling down and down the toilet further since T2 and there is no reason or need for sequels after that. I didn't mind Salvation (in it's director's cut only and not the butchered theatrical cut) because it's a prequel to the first movie and didn't connect to T2 or trying to ruin the storyline of T2, every sequel that has came out after T2 has went out to destroy the mythos and destroy the story. Made it not really have a lasting impact, i mean Terminator 2 is still an awesome and great sequel don't get me wrong! but it's hurt by the existence of T3, the tv show and Genesis and this new movie.

It ended beautifully with T2! judgement day was stopped, a terminator saved the world from the apocalypse that was gonna be created by his own kind and it was fitting, it ended with a thumbs up and we feel sorry for John Conner at the end. Plus John and Sarah moved on the road to be somewhere else. It was the best way to end the franchise and story, all loose ends were tied up. Everything was totally 100 percent closed when it comes to the story. Then comes T3, which has a lazy bullcrap storyline that it throws out the "no no no Judgement day was inevitable! it doesn't matter the sacrifice of Dyson", sequels were inevitable! this was a popular franchise and there was no way in hell that would wind up with the rights to the Terminator franchise and went to milk it for every last drop.

That's what's going on here! and that's too bad. If you ask me, this franchise should be put in the crusher, be crushed and be terminated. It really needed to have the plug pulled on it long before now as after T2, it's been nothing but diminishing returns. Hopefully this tanks, or underperforms in theaters and that kills the franchise for good and give studios the message because it would be for the franchise's benefit. Let's move on to fresh new cinematic franchises like John Wick which took risks and became a hit franchise which i'm glad audiences are supporting a new cinematic franchise like that where some worn out ones like Terminator need to die.

The franchise is like having a friend you love in the first 2 films but yet if you didn't mind part 4 in it's director's cut for being a prequel to the first movie but for a long time been in a vegetative state on life support since T2, he's been suffering and you've come to accept he's gone. Your waiting for someone to pull the plug and turn off the machine.

Everything that happened in the second movie, after Sarah wakes up and decides to stop Skynet created a new future. Last Jedi received great reviews from both critics and audiences alike. Check the Rotten Tomato score. Lol. I know you wanted Last Jedi to fail but fact is it was critically acclaimed.

TLJ got mixed reviews from fans and on a personal note, I think it's a weak film that is just a weak retread of The Empire Strike Back and doesn't hold a candle to Dark Force Rising. Though worst Sci-Fi film ever made? No, not ever close. The acting is solid and the directing is competent. As for Terminator Dark Fate, Everyone knows this is the series last chance to prove itself. Dark Fate has a great cast, a critically acclaimed director, and James Cameron returning to give this film the best shot it can have.

And so far, most fans (who aren't idiots who insist that women ruin everything because they have cooties) are on board for Dark Fate. Most reaction videos have been positive and major fan sites have been open minded about the film due to the talent behind it.

There are issues I can understand, like the Rev9 spending most of its screen time getting beaten up and not doing much to show itself as a competent threat. Dani getting next to no dialogue and her always being saved in every trailer (though to be fair the same thing happened with Sarah in Terminator 1's trailers (yeah, the more things change)). And that it being just a retread of the first two films plot.

These are issues I get and even agree with but I'm willing to give Dark Fate a chance because there's no way it can be worse then Genisys. You want a shit Sci-Fi movie, Genisys is it. The action is dull and generic, the plot is a convoluted mess, and the characters are either forgettable or annoying or, in the case of Genisys-Kyle Reese, somehow Both! Genisys is such a low point for me that it the series really can only go up from there. The only way ANY Terminator film can be worse then Genisys is if Every character was somehow replaced with Genisys-Kyle.

Well, here's a question, seeing that it's still seen as the best in the series and one of the greatest films/sequels of all time if Star Trek had stopped after The Wrath of Khan, which WAS intended to be the last Trek film due to the poor reception of Star Trek The Motion Picture would the series have been better for it?

I mean most fans would agree that Wrath is the best but many would also argue that the series as a whole would be lesser without The Voyager Home, The Next Generation, The Undiscovered Country, First Contact, and Deep Space 9. All came out after what is often considered The Gold Standard that was the intended finally of the series and all are considered classics in their own right.

Will Dark Fate be seen as good as T2. Doubtful. Does it have a chance of being good in it's own right and maybe even be seen as a classic. Maybe. I mean Star Trek: The Final Frontier was a bad BAD movie but after that we got what many consider to be the second best film in the series The Undiscovered Country.

Dark Fate has a good cast, a solid director and several talented writers, one of who is the creator of the much beloved Sarah Connor Chronicles whom I'm pretty sure was the one who came up with the idea of a Human-Terminator Hybrid. T3 through Genisys had writers and directors who were, at best, okay and none of them knew what to do with the series. Miller is at least working with Cameron on this film, the writers actually give damn about making this the best film they can so there is hope.

Tell me, what's the difference between a Latino-American Terminator slaughtering Boarder Patrol agents to get at one target and a Austrian Terminator slaughtering Police Officers or a Causation-American Terminator killing Police Officers to get at a child.

Terminator, and James Cameron's work as a whole, has never been pro-authority. Remember, Terminator was born out of fears of a nuclear war because of the Cold War and T2 likewise was still part of that fear and how it could still happen. Also, your character icon, Superman, was a metaphor for Jewish Immigration and that was intentional on the part of his creators, who were Jewish.

Stories in EVERY media is shaped by the world around it and 10 times out of 10 that includes politics. Hell, look at one of the most well known storytellers of all time, William Shakespeare. Richard III, Macbeth, The Henry Saga, and Hamlet, are either complete political propaganda (the latter 3) or meant to make the then current ruling party look better by comparison. Even Romeo and Juliet has this as the most level headed and reasonable character, The Prince, was likely meant as a stand in for the royals of Shakespeare's times.

Macbeth is known as a tragic villain who's ego and lust for power led to his downfall while the Real Macbeth was, by all accounts, a noble and fair ruler who was only cast as the villain in the play because his enemy was the current Royal line.

And it doesn't stop there, The Dark Knight Returns, Horizon Zero Dawn, The Last of Us, Harry Potter, The Witcher, Star Wars well before Disney got a hold of it and, again, Superman, all have some sort of political "agenda" that was meant to talk about political issues of their times. That extends to such stories as The Wizard of Oz who's original creator included a BUNCH of LGBT undertones like characters turning from one gender to another AND more or less questioning their sexual identity. 

So Dark Fate is nothing new when it comes to being politically driven as literally Every Story Is In The End. Yes, it's got a Latino woman as the new chosen one, two of her guardians are women one of whom may very well be her love interest, and it does include the Border Patrol who are killed by a Terminator who appears as a Latino-American.

Ghostbusters: Answer the Call was a bland movie that will be forgotten in time, Wonder Woman is a great movie that will likely stand the test of time and BOTH are politically driven and yes, meant to reflect politics of our time. What made one bland and forgettable is that there was no effort put into it while the other had a great deal of effort put into it so Dark Fate's, um, fate will depend on how much effort everyone puts into it. And at least with Dark Fate, unlike Genisys everyone does seem to be bringing their A Game.

Dani girl here at 1:30 carries a Standard Manufacturing DP-12  pump-action shotgun that weights 1/10 her own weight like is a nerf gun. Not to mention that she barely blinks after shooting, which makes no sense given the recoil power of this shotgun.

Given that Sarah Conner has direct, firsthand experience of a Terminator not only protecting her son and SACRIFICING ITSELF to change the future to protect all of humanity (something she conveniently fails to give him credit for [or Miles Dyson's sacrifice, or anything her own son did for that matter] when she says SHE changed the future), why would she automatically attack a terminator that her allies brought her to for help.  Wouldn't it occur to her that her allies brought her here for a reason?  And why promise to destroy it when this was all over?  She KNOWS that a Terminator CAN be a good guy.  In fact that the realization that a terminator, of all things, could do this was a major epiphany for her at the end of T2, and gave her back the hope that the bleak years preparing for judgment day had sapped from her.  This is the kind of thing that annoys me so much with sequels -- the writers studiously ignore character development and reset a character's attitudes and opinions back to an earlier point for no logical reason except, uh, tension or drama or something.

@sly8961 If so, that's just as much of a problem.  The hero T800 sacrificed itself at the end of T2 to prevent its technology being used to restart the Skynet program.  How is another one schlepping around circa 1991?  Who sent it?  What was it sent for?

@sly8961 Okay, that's just making things even worse.  It raises more questions than it answers.  Why would Skynet send a T800 after they averted judgment day.  What would be the use at that point.  More importantly, HOW would Skynet send a T800 to kill John Connor after judgment day has been averted?  It was AVERTED; it's too late now, there she BE no Skynet.  Why would Skynet, after sending the super-advanced go back to using an obsolete model?  What possible reason would Sarah Connor have for reprogramming the T800 and keeping it alive?  If she's been willing to let it live this long, why is she so hot to wax it now?  Especially when it can offer useful assistance.


Ugh... This movie is looking more and more like a trainwreck that ever before.

@sly8961 Yeah, they're pretty underwhelming.  And it still amounts to yet ANOTHER retcon, as John Connor was supposed to grow up to become a senator in an unused scene presented on the special edition release.  I suppose, since it didn't make it into the final cut, that's not canon, but still...


Some heroes really SHOULD be able to just ride off into the sunset, and I would argue that John Connor was one of them, rather than milk the same old concepts for a series that's gone on long past its sell by date.  None of the movies since Terminator 3 has risen above mediocre, and Genysis was simply awful.  Terminator 2 was a terrific film, with a perfect ending.  Pity they couldn't leave it there if this was the best they could come up with.

During the theatrical ending for Terminator 2 (not the director's cut ending) Sarah mentions how they changed the fate of the world but still don't know what lies ahead in their future. The beginning of this trailer where she says "August 29th, 1997 was supposed to be Judgement Day..." implies that they did indeed prevent that specific date from being Judgement Day but it still happened later anyways. 

Machines are walking up on the beach among the iron X-beams like troops landing at Normandy as she says that line. This is supposed to draw a parallel with D-Day, but it falls flat on it's face because the Allies, not the Nazis, were the ones walking up the beach during that battle. Basically, that shot implies the Nazis were the good guys in World War 2 and the Americans were the bad guys, and surely that was not their intended message because Hollywood would never greenlight such a project. Whoever made this garbage has no clue how to tell a story or even get the most basic type of message across to an audience.

In other words, this shit sucks.

I don't understand what Hollywood's fixation is with taking niche franchises and trying to make them all "family friendly"... I mean I understand the basic concept, a larger market means more money to draw upon, but the process just winds up diluting the source material to the point of interpretive ice dance style mediocrity. I mean I would respectfully had john connor there, actually finding out that the new "john connor" is a key player to stop the new skynet and becomes one of the protectors, and being able to teach her after most of the big names go hasta la vista. So if you just kill connor, why not kill him in some sort of battle, and die a legend than some myth that was erased. I am 17 years old and I don't relate to my culture. Why be neutral than pick a side for a message. Terminator 1 gave us a very good look at a bleak future with killer robots, Terminator 2 gave us new action and new heroes and villains to enjoy. But what made those different from those films than this film. They had no message about politics, they had messages about what if an Artificial Intellegence became our enemy, designed killer robots to after us, and our only goal was to assault the AI, and travel back in time to protect that promise of a future. Dark Fate fans, why do you like it? Is it because of actors? Or because of story or the message? I pose this to you:

Actors are usually there for money and fame, movies are meant to escape not to tell a message. What makes John Carpenter's They Live enjoyable and still have a message is that he was doing a satire of what if the bad guys were aliens and they were manipulating us. No democratic presidential Republican bull crap, just a satire on reaganomics. Movies can tell a message but it mustn't become part of the plot. If they made let's say, they did a sequel to your favorite film and throw out let's say 5/10 main characters and it was wanting to be a re hash of other parts of the film, would you still like the movie if it also added that autistic people were the bad guys. Look I have Autism and ADHD and I hate it when people say things that make autistic people look bad, but do you know anyone that's autistic and is able to right a response to a trailer of a upcoming movie that is from a great franchise now relying on 3 original actors, the original creator and the guy who brought us back a smart mouthed hero. I end with this question:

If you have a favorite movie and it was being remade with a bunch of messages that is going on in society, would you still like the film?
